context:
Possible response actions: LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS, LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD, NONE, CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND, LIST_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE, LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, CALENDAR_ACTION, BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE, REPLY, RELEASE_BLOCK, BLOCK_APPS, START_REMOTE_SESSION, LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, SCHEDULING, GENERATE_DOSSIER, REMOTE_DESKTOP, PASSWORD_MANAGER, IGNORE, REVOKE_REMOTE_SESSION, SCREEN_TIME, CALL_EXTERNAL, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, X_READ, UPDATE_ENTITY, UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE, RELATIONSHIP, LIFE, REQUEST_FIELD_FILL, INBOX, CHECK_AVAILABILITY, DOSSIER, GET_APP_BLOCK_STATUS, REGISTER_BROWSER_SESSION, MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER, CALENDLY, SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS, GET_TIME_ON_APP, FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT, RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN, GET_TIME_ON_SITE, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, RUN_MORNING_CHECKIN, INTENT_SYNC, HEALTH, CALL_USER, GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, UNBLOCK_APPS, UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, BLOCK_WEBSITES, SUBSCRIPTIONS, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER, TWILIO_VOICE_CALL, PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES

# Available Actions
actions[58]:
- UNBLOCK_APPS: Admin-only. Remove the current app block, unshielding all blocked apps.
  aliases[4]: UNBLOCK_APP, REMOVE_APP_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_APPS, UNSHIELD_APPS
  example: User: "Unblock my apps." -> actions: UNBLOCK_APPS
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN: Run the night check-in: review today's meetings, completed wins, and any overdue todos for the owner. Use this for a dedicated evening wrap-up, not for inbox daily briefs, unread cross-channel summaries, or reply workflows.
  aliases[3]: NIGHT_CHECKIN, START_NIGHT_CHECKIN, EVENING_CHECKIN
- NONE: Respond but perform no additional action. This is the default if the agent is speaking and not doing anything additional.
  aliases[5]: NO_ACTION, NO_RESPONSE, NO_REACTION, NOOP, PASS
  example: User: "Hey whats up" -> actions: NONE
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- INTENT_SYNC: Broadcast intents across devices or acknowledge pending intents. Subactions: broadcast, list_pending, acknowledge, prune_expired.
  aliases[3]: BROADCAST_INTENT, SYNC_INTENT, CROSS_DEVICE_INTENT
  params[12]: subaction:string [values=broadcast|list_pending|acknowledge|prune_expired] - Which operation to perform. One of: broadcast, list_pending, acknowledge, prune_expired.; intent?:string - User-phrased intent summary (optional, for logging).; kind?:string [values=user_action_requested|routine_reminder|attention_request|state_sync] - Intent kind. One of: user_action_requested, routine_reminder, attention_request, state_sync.; title?:string - Short intent title (shown in notification).; body?:string - Intent body text.; target?:string [values=all|desktop|mobile|specific] - Target device class. One of: all, desktop, mobile, specific. Defaults to "all".; targetDeviceId?:string - Specific device id when target = 'specific'.; actionUrl?:string - Deep link URL for mobile follow-up.; priority?:string [values=low|medium|high|urgent] - Priority. One of: low, medium, high, urgent. Defaults to "medium".; intentId?:string - Intent id to acknowledge.; deviceId?:string - Device id performing the acknowledgement, or requesting pending intents.; expiresInMinutes?:number - Expire the intent after this many minutes.
  example: User: "Ping my phone to take out the trash" -> actions: INTENT_SYNC
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser. Use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead for Finder/Desktop automation, screenshots, folder creation, or local file workflows on this machine.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE: Mark a contact as already followed-up-with (updates lastContactedAt to now). Use this only when the interaction already happened, not for future reminders. Requires either an explicit contactId (UUID) or an unambiguous contactName. Ambiguous names return a clarifying response without modifying any contact.
  aliases[5]: FOLLOWED_UP, FOLLOWUP_DONE, CONTACTED, MARK_CONTACTED, RECORD_INTERACTION
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known — eliminates name ambiguity.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; note?:string - Optional note about the interaction.
  example: User: "I just talked to Alice, mark it done" -> actions: MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE
- BLOCK_APPS: Admin-only. Block selected apps on the user's phone using native OS controls. On iPhone, uses Family Controls to shield apps. On Android, uses Usage Access to detect and overlay blocked apps. Use this for requests like 'block all games on my phone until 6pm' or 'block the Slack app while I focus on deep work'. Pass app package names (Android) or previously selected app tokens (iPhone) to block.
  aliases[6]: BLOCK_APP, BLOCK_APPLICATION, APP_BLOCKER, START_APP_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_APPS, SHIELD_APPS
  params[3]: packageNames?:array of string - Android package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android', 'com.instagram.android']. Not used on iPhone.; appTokens?:array of string - iPhone app tokens from a previous selectApps() call. Not used on Android.; durationMinutes?:number - How long to block the apps, in minutes. Omit for indefinite block.
  example: User: "Block Twitter and Instagram for 2 hours." -> actions: BLOCK_APPS
- PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT: Publish a cross-device intent (alarm, reminder, block, or custom) to the device bus so all paired devices can realize it. Use this for desktop+phone reminder ladders, multi-device meeting nudges, document-signing reminders, and urgent device-level escalation where the owner wants the same intent realized across paired devices. Standing 'if/when this happens, warn or remind me on my devices' policies should still use this action on the first turn.
  aliases[6]: BROADCAST_DEVICE_INTENT, FIRE_DEVICE_INTENT, NOTIFY_ALL_DEVICES, SIGNATURE_REMINDER, MEETING_REMINDER_LADDER, DEVICE_WARNING
  params[3]: kind?:string - Intent kind. Standard values: alarm, reminder, block. Custom string values accepted.; payload?:object - Opaque JSON payload describing the intent (time, label, duration, url, etc.).; userId?:string - Override which user's paired devices should receive the intent. Defaults to the owner.
  example: User: "The clinic sent docs for me to sign before the appointment. Keep me on top of that." -> actions: PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT
- LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE: Control the owner's desktop (screenshots, mouse, keyboard, browser, windows, files, terminal) via @elizaos/plugin-computeruse. Use this for portal uploads, Finder/Desktop tasks like creating folders or taking screenshots, browser form-filling, and other on-machine workflows the assistant should perform directly, including standing instructions like 'when I send the file, upload it to the portal for me.' Owner-only. Disabled when ELIZA_LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE_ENABLED=0.
  aliases[12]: USE_COMPUTER, DESKTOP_AUTOMATION, COMPUTER_USE, CONTROL_DESKTOP, FINDER, OPEN_FINDER, CREATE_FOLDER, NEW_FOLDER, TAKE_SCREENSHOT, CAPTURE_SCREEN, PORTAL_UPLOAD, UPLOAD_DECK
  params[10]: surface?:string [values=desktop|browser|window|file|terminal] - Computer-use surface to route to. Use browser, file, terminal, window, or desktop when the operation is ambiguous.; action?:string - Underlying computer-use action name, such as screenshot, navigate, read, execute, or focus.; url?:string - Browser target URL.; selector?:string - Browser selector for click/type/wait.; path?:string - Filesystem path for file operations.; command?:string - Terminal command for shell execution.; windowId?:string - Window target for window-management operations.; coordinate?:array of number - Desktop or browser coordinate [x, y].; text?:string - Text payload for typing, OCR, or browser waits.; key?:string - Desktop key or key combo.
  example: User: "Take a screenshot of my desktop" -> actions: LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE
- BLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Start a local website block by editing the system hosts file. Use this for fixed-duration or generic focus blocks like 'block twitter and reddit for the next 2 hours', 'turn on a focus block for all social media sites', or 'block youtube'. Use recent conversation context to block public websites like x.com for a fixed duration or until manually unblocked. Do not use this when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, or todo; that is BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE. Always drafts first; the owner must pass confirmed: true (e.g. by replying 'confirm') to actually edit the hosts file. If the user confirms a block in a follow-up message without repeating the hostnames, reuse that context through the action planner. DO NOT use this action when the user references apps, games, or things 'on my phone' / 'on my device' — use BLOCK_APPS for those. Do not pair this action with a speculative REPLY; this action provides the final reply itself.
  aliases[8]: SELFCONTROL_BLOCK_WEBSITES, BLOCK_WEBSITE, BLOCK_SITE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_NOW, WEBSITE_BLOCKER, WEBSITEBLOCKER, START_FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_SITES
  params[3]: websites?:array of string - Website hostnames or URLs to block, for example ['x.com', 'twitter.com']. When omitted, the planner can recover them from recent conversation context.; durationMinutes?:number [default=60] - How long to block those websites, in minutes. Omit this to use the default duration.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, the action returns a draft confirmation request instead of editing the system hosts file.
  example: User: "Block x.com and twitter.com for 2 hours." -> actions: BLOCK_WEBSITES
- CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND: Draft or send a message across any connected channel (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, notifications). Always drafts first; caller must re-invoke with confirmed: true to dispatch.
  aliases[4]: SEND_MESSAGE_TO, DRAFT_MESSAGE, SEND_ACROSS_CHANNEL, SEND_MESSAGE
  params[5]: channel:string [values=email|telegram|discord|signal|sms|twilio_voice|imessage|whatsapp|notifications|calendly|x_dm] - Channel to send on. One of: email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, notifications, calendly, x_dm.; target:string - Recipient identifier. Email address for email, E.164 phone for sms/twilio_voice, handle/user ID for chat channels, Ntfy topic name for notifications.; message:string - Message body (plaintext).; subject?:string - Email subject (email channel only).; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true to actually dispatch. Otherwise returns a draft preview.
  example: User: "Email alice@example.com the meeting notes" -> actions: CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND
- SUBSCRIPTIONS: Audit recurring subscriptions from LifeOps signals, cancel supported subscriptions through the browser, and report cancellation status with artifacts and human-handoff states. Use this for requests like 'cancel my Netflix subscription', 'cancel Hulu in my browser', 'cancel my Google Play subscription', or 'cancel my App Store subscription on this Mac'.
  aliases[8]: SUBSCRIPTION_AUDIT, SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL, UNSUBSCRIBE_SERVICE, MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS, CANCEL_NETFLIX, CANCEL_HULU, CANCEL_APP_STORE_SUBSCRIPTION, CANCEL_GOOGLE_PLAY_SUBSCRIPTION
  example: User: "Audit my subscriptions and tell me what I can cancel." -> actions: SUBSCRIPTIONS
- REMOTE_DESKTOP: Start or end a secure remote desktop session so you can view/control the computer from another device. Requires a pairing code. Use this only for remote-session lifecycle work (start, status, end, list), not for local Finder/Desktop automation, screenshots, browser workflows, or file handling on this machine — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[4]: REMOTE_SESSION, VNC_SESSION, REMOTE_CONTROL, PHONE_REMOTE_ACCESS
  params[4]: subaction:string - One of: start, status, end, list.; intent?:string - Freeform owner intent / reason for the session. Logged for audit.; sessionId?:string - Session id required for status and end subactions.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be true to actually start a remote session (security sensitive).
  example: User: "Open a remote desktop so I can help from my phone" -> actions: REMOTE_DESKTOP
- START_REMOTE_SESSION: Open a remote-control session. Requires confirmed: true. If running in local mode (MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1) no pairing code is needed; otherwise a valid 6-digit pairing code is required. Use this only to start a remote session, not for local screenshots, Finder/Desktop tasks, or browser/file automation on this Mac — those belong to LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE.
  aliases[3]: OPEN_REMOTE_SESSION, BEGIN_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_START
  params[3]: pairingCode?:string - 6-digit one-time pairing code. Required unless MILADY_REMOTE_LOCAL_MODE=1.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to actually start the session.; requesterIdentity?:string - Identifier for who is asking (entity id, friend name, device id). Logged for audit.
  example: User: "Start a remote session with code 482193" -> actions: START_REMOTE_SESSION
- X_READ: Read X/Twitter DMs, the home timeline or mentions feed, or run a recent search. Use this for requests like 'check my Twitter DMs', 'what's on my X timeline?', 'show me my mentions', or 'search Twitter for posts about elizaOS'. Use this for retrieving content from X, not posting. Do not reply that X/Twitter access is unavailable when this action is registered and visible.
  aliases[10]: READ_X, READ_TWITTER, CHECK_TWITTER_DMS, TWITTER_TIMELINE, X_TIMELINE, TWITTER_MENTIONS, SEARCH_TWITTER, X_DMS, X_FEED, X_SEARCH
  params[5]: subaction?:string [values=read_dms|read_feed|search] - X read operation. read_dms for direct messages, read_feed for timeline/mentions, search for recent tweet search.; intent?:string - Free-text description of the request, e.g. "check my X DMs", "show my mentions", "search X for elizaOS".; query?:string - Search query string for the search subaction.; feedType?:string [values=home_timeline|mentions|search] - Feed to read for read_feed. One of home_timeline (default), mentions, or search.; limit?:number - Max items to return (1-100).
  example: User: "Check my X DMs." -> actions: X_READ
- GENERATE_DOSSIER: Low-level event-specific dossier generation for an already identified calendar event. Use this only when the event id or fuzzy event title is already known from a prior workflow. Prefer DOSSIER for generic user requests like 'brief me', 'background on the person I'm meeting', or 'prep me for my next meeting'.
  aliases[3]: MEETING_DOSSIER_BY_EVENT, EVENT_DOSSIER, GENERATE_DOSSIER_FROM_EVENT
  params[3]: eventId?:string - Calendar event id. Mutually optional with eventTitleFuzzy.; eventTitleFuzzy?:string - Fuzzy title match used when eventId is unknown (e.g. 'my 3pm').; windowDays?:number - Context window in days (default 7).
  example: User: "Generate a dossier for event 'Board sync tomorrow'" -> actions: GENERATE_DOSSIER
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS: List all currently active website block rules, including their gate type and gate target. Only use this for website/app blocking status. Do not use it for inbox blockers, message priority, daily briefs, or general executive-assistant triage.
  aliases[3]: LIST_BLOCK_RULES, SHOW_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, WEBSITE_BLOCKS_STATUS
  example: User: "What website blocks are active right now?" -> actions: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- REVOKE_REMOTE_SESSION: Revoke an active remote-control session by id.
  aliases[3]: END_REMOTE_SESSION, CLOSE_REMOTE_SESSION, REMOTE_REVOKE
  params[1]: sessionId:string - Session id returned by START_REMOTE_SESSION.
  example: User: "End the remote session" -> actions: REVOKE_REMOTE_SESSION
- REQUEST_FIELD_FILL: Ask the LifeOps browser extension to autofill a field via the installed password manager (1Password or ProtonPass). Refuses on domains not in the user's autofill whitelist. Credentials never pass through the agent — the extension resolves them locally.
  aliases[3]: AUTOFILL_FIELD, AUTOFILL_REQUEST, FILL_PASSWORD_FIELD
  params[4]: tabUrl?:string - URL of the tab where the field should be filled. Used for whitelist enforcement.; fieldPurpose?:string - One of: email, password, name, phone, custom. Tells the password manager which field to resolve.; fieldSelector?:string - Optional CSS selector narrowing which field to fill on the page.; customKey?:string - When fieldPurpose is 'custom', the key in the password-manager item to resolve (e.g. 'API key').
- SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD: Set a recurring follow-up cadence threshold (in days) for a specific contact. Use this for durable rules like 'every 14 days', not one-off reminders like 'next week'. Requires a positive integer threshold and either contactId or an unambiguous contactName.
  aliases[3]: FOLLOWUP_RULE, CHANGE_FOLLOWUP_INTERVAL, SET_CONTACT_FREQUENCY_DAYS
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; thresholdDays:number - Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.
  example: User: "Remind me to follow up with Dana every 14 days" -> actions: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
- LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS: List currently active remote-control sessions.
  aliases[3]: REMOTE_SESSIONS, ACTIVE_REMOTE_SESSIONS, REMOTE_LIST
  example: User: "Are any remote sessions open?" -> actions: LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS
- LIST_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: List effective autofill whitelist entries: the bundled defaults plus user-added entries.
  aliases[2]: SHOW_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, GET_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Use this for real phone calls, not for calendar-only rescheduling or advice. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[6]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_SUPPORT
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- CHECK_AVAILABILITY: Check whether the owner is free or busy across a specific ISO-8601 time window. Returns a free/busy summary and any overlapping events.
  aliases[3]: AM_I_FREE, AVAILABILITY_CHECK, FREE_BUSY
  params[2]: startAt:string - ISO-8601 start of the window to check.; endAt:string - ISO-8601 end of the window to check.
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.
  aliases[7]: SET_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SAVE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SET_PREFERRED_TIMES, SET_BLACKOUT_WINDOWS, SLEEP_WINDOW, NO_CALL_HOURS, PROTECT_SLEEP

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

rules[20]:
- think briefly, then respond
- always include a <thought> field, even for direct replies
- actions execute in listed order
- if replying without another grounded state/action query, REPLY goes first
- use IGNORE or STOP only by themselves
- include providers only when needed
- when the user asks about uploaded files, documents, prior uploads, or knowledge-base contents, call the relevant providers before replying instead of asking the user to resend the material
- when the user refers to "the uploaded file", "the document I uploaded", or a prior upload without naming it, treat that as a provider lookup request first; only ask which file after grounded document/knowledge lookup still leaves multiple plausible answers
- use provider_hints from context when present instead of restating the same rules
- if an action needs inputs, include them inside that action's <params> block
- if a required param is unknown, ask for clarification in text
- for live status questions or remaining-work queries, do not answer from recent conversation alone; call the relevant action/provider to refresh state, and do not pair it with a speculative REPLY that guesses the result
- when an action will fetch the state and produce the final grounded answer, do not add REPLY just to say "checking", "let me look", or similar filler; use the action alone and leave text empty
- when the user asks you to create, store, remember, schedule, remind, upload, follow up, route, escalate, or set a standing policy, choose the matching action instead of handling it in prose only
- for standing or future-condition requests like "if/when X, do Y", still choose the action that records, queues, or routes that behavior on the first turn
- if a matching action can own the task and ask the missing follow-up itself, still select that action and put the clarification in text; do not reply in prose alone
- when the user defines a durable preference, recurring block, escalation policy, upload policy, approval-gated workflow, or multi-device reminder rule, select the owning action even if some implementation details are still missing
- do not wait for portal names, priority labels, event IDs, exact travel preferences, or the definition of "important" before selecting the owning action; let the action gather those details
- for LifeOps create requests with a clear defaultable habit or natural window, such as drinking water, stretch breaks during the day, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, or brushing when waking up and before bed, call LIFE instead of asking for exact clock times unless the user explicitly asks for precise scheduling
- only choose actions that directly satisfy the user's request or an explicit live-state question; do not opportunistically triage inboxes, summarize calendars, propose meetings, or call adjacent tools just because provider context makes them available
- when the user is venting, reflecting, stating an opinion, or asking for generic advice about a domain, stay in REPLY or NONE unless they explicitly ask you to inspect state, change state, send something, schedule something, or perform a real operation

control_actions:
- STOP means the task is done and the agent should end the run without executing more actions
- STOP is a terminal control action even if it is not listed in available actions

fields[5]{name,meaning}:
- thought | short plan
- actions | ordered <action> entries inside <actions>
- providers | array of provider names, or empty
- text | next message for TestAgent
- simple | true or false

formatting:
- wrap multi-line code in fenced code blocks
- use inline backticks for short code identifiers
<output>
Return only XML. No prose before or after it. No <think>.




Use this shape:
<response>
  <thought>Your internal reasoning about the message and what to do</thought>
  <actions>Ordered action entries. For XML, use one or more <action><name>ACTION_NAME</name><params>...</params></action> blocks inside <actions>.</actions>
  <providers>Optional provider names to call before the final reply or action. Use an empty field when no provider lookup is needed.</providers>
  <text>The text response to send to the user</text>
  <simple>Whether this is a simple response (true/false)</simple>
</response>


Return exactly one <response>...</response>.
</output>

  <thought>Based on the message "show my step count today", we need to call the HEALTH action with subaction "today" to get the user's step count for the current day.</thought>

      <name>HEALTH</name>
      <params>
        <subaction>today</subaction>
      </params>
    </action>
  </actions>
  <providers></providers>
  <text></text>
  <simple>false</simple>
</response>

llmCalls.prompt:
Plan the BACKGROUND JOB action for job kind: daily_brief.
You are routing a background tick for an executive assistant.
Decide whether the assistant should take an action right now, and if so
which action and through which channel. The assistant MUST NOT execute
sensitive actions directly — anything that contacts a person, modifies a
calendar, books travel, makes a call, runs a workflow, or spends money
returns requiresApproval=true so the user can confirm.

Return ONLY valid JSON with exactly these fields:
{"action":"send_message"|"send_email"|"schedule_event"|"modify_event"|"cancel_event"|"book_travel"|"make_call"|"execute_workflow"|"spend_money"|"noop",
"channel":"telegram"|"discord"|"slack"|"imessage"|"sms"|"email"|"google_calendar"|"browser"|"phone"|"internal",
"requiresApproval":true|false,
"reason":"short justification",
"payload":{...action-specific fields, or empty object when action=noop}}

Rules:
- Choose action=noop when no action is warranted this tick (e.g. nothing overdue).
- requiresApproval=true for any action that touches a person or external system.
- requiresApproval=false ONLY when action=noop or the action is purely internal (logging, internal workflow with no side effects).
- The reason must explain WHY this tick warrants the chosen action, in any language.

Since this action is internal and does not touch any external system or person, `requiresApproval` is set to `false`. The reason is provided to justify why this tick warrants the chosen action.

ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION

- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.

- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.

Use this only for an existing proposal workflow: start a negotiation record, submit a concrete proposal for that negotiation, record accepted/declined responses, finalize the winning proposal, cancel, or list negotiations/proposals. # Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL

Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. # Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

rules[11]:
- think briefly, then continue the task from the latest action results
- actions execute in listed order
- if replying, REPLY goes first
- use IGNORE or STOP only by themselves
- include providers only when needed
- when the user asks about uploaded files, documents, prior uploads, or knowledge-base contents, call the relevant providers before replying instead of asking the user to resend the material
- when the user refers to "the uploaded file", "the document I uploaded", or a prior upload without naming it, treat that as a provider lookup request first; only ask which file after grounded document/knowledge lookup still leaves multiple plausible answers
- use provider_hints from context when present instead of restating the same rules
- if an action needs inputs, include them under params keyed by action name
- if a required param is unknown, ask for clarification in text
- if reflection says the task is incomplete, keep working or explain the concrete follow-up you still need
- if the task is complete, either reply to the user or use STOP to end the run
- STOP is a terminal control action even if it is not listed in available actions

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY

Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. - CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION

- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.

- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.

- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

rules[11]:
- think briefly, then continue the task from the latest action results
- actions execute in listed order
- if replying, REPLY goes first
- use IGNORE or STOP only by themselves
- include providers only when needed
- when the user asks about uploaded files, documents, prior uploads, or knowledge-base contents, call the relevant providers before replying instead of asking the user to resend the material
- when the user refers to "the uploaded file", "the document I uploaded", or a prior upload without naming it, treat that as a provider lookup request first; only ask which file after grounded document/knowledge lookup still leaves multiple plausible answers
- use provider_hints from context when present instead of restating the same rules
- if an action needs inputs, include them under params keyed by action name
- if a required param is unknown, ask for clarification in text
- if reflection says the task is incomplete, keep working or explain the concrete follow-up you still need
- if the task is complete, either reply to the user or use STOP to end the run
- STOP is a terminal control action even if it is not listed in available actions

Always drafts first; the owner must pass confirmed: true (e.g. by replying 'confirm') to actually edit the hosts file. If the user confirms a block in a follow-up message without repeating the hostnames, reuse that context through the action planner. DO NOT use this action when the user references apps, games, or things 'on my phone' / 'on my device' — use BLOCK_APPS for those. Do not pair this action with a speculative REPLY; this action provides the final reply itself.
  aliases[8]: SELFCONTROL_BLOCK_WEBSITES, BLOCK_WEBSITE, BLOCK_SITE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_NOW, WEBSITE_BLOCKER, WEBSITEBLOCKER, START_FOCUS_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_SITES
  params[3]: websites?:array of string - Website hostnames or URLs to block, for example ['x.com', 'twitter.com']. When omitted, the planner can recover them from recent conversation context.; durationMinutes?:number [default=60] - How long to block those websites, in minutes. Omit this to use the default duration.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, the action returns a draft confirmation request instead of editing the system hosts file.
  example: User: "Block x.com and twitter.com for 2 hours." -> actions: BLOCK_WEBSITES

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL

Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION

- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.

- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

**Health Action Plan**

**Language Support**

The system will support any language, and the response will be in the user's language.

**JSON Response Format**

The system will always return a valid JSON response with exactly the following fields:

* `subaction` (string): one of "today", "trend", "by_metric", "status", or null
* `metric` (string): one of "steps", "heart_rate", "sleep_hours", "calories", "distance_meters", "active_minutes", or null
* `days` (number or null): a positive integer representing the number of days, or null if not specified
* `shouldAct` (boolean): true if the user's request is specific enough, false otherwise
* `response` (string or null): a clarifying question if `shouldAct` is false, or null otherwise

**Subaction Logic**

* If the user asks about health backend connection or availability, set `subaction` to "status".
* If the user asks for fitness/health activity over a window of days, a week, or recent history, set `subaction` to "trend".
* If the user names a specific metric and wants its current or recent value, set `subaction` to "by_metric".
* If the user asks for today's overall summary, set `subaction` to "today" (default).
* If the user's request is too vague to choose any subaction, set `shouldAct` to false and provide a clarifying question in the user's language.

**metric and days Logic**

* If `subaction` is "by_metric", `metric` must be one of the listed enum values.
* If `subaction` is not "by_metric", `metric` must be null.
* If the user implies a specific number of days (e.g., "this week" for 7 days), set `days` to that number.
* If the user does not specify a number of days, set `days` to null.

**Example Responses**

* "How many steps did I take today?" -> {"subaction":"today","metric":null,"days":null,"shouldAct":true,"response":null}
* "Show me my fitness trend this week" -> {"subaction":"trend","metric":null,"days":7,"shouldAct":true,"response":null}
* "Tell me my heart rate" -> {"subaction":"by_metric","metric":"heart_rate","days":null,"shouldAct":true,"response":null}
* "Is my health integration connected?" -> {"subaction":"status","metric":null,"days":null,"shouldAct":true,"response":null}
* "¿Cuántos pasos di hoy?" -> {"subaction":"today","metric":null,"days":null,"shouldAct":true,"response":null}

**Implementation**

The system will use natural language processing (NLP) techniques to analyze the user's request and determine the subaction, metric, and days. If the request is too vague, the system will set `shouldAct` to false and provide a clarifying question in the user's language.

Here is a sample implementation in Python:
```python
import json

def health_action(request):
    # Analyze the user's request using NLP techniques
    intent = analyze_request(request)
    structured_parameters = {}
    recent_conversation = get_recent_conversation()

    # Determine the subaction
    if intent == "show my step count today":
        subaction = "today"
    elif intent == "show my fitness trend this week":
        subaction = "trend"
        structured_parameters["days"] = 7
    elif intent == "tell me my heart rate":
        subaction = "by_metric"
        structured_parameters["metric"] = "heart_rate"
    elif intent == "is my health integration connected?":
        subaction = "status"
    else:
        subaction = None

    # Determine the metric and days
    if subaction == "by_metric":
        structured_parameters["metric"] = "heart_rate"
    else:
        structured_parameters["metric"] = None

    if structured_parameters.get("days") is None:
        structured_parameters["days"] = None

    # Determine shouldAct and response
    if subaction is None:
        structured_parameters["shouldAct"] = False
        structured_parameters["response"] = "Could you please be more specific?"
    else:
        structured_parameters["shouldAct"] = True
        structured_parameters["response"] = None

    # Return the JSON response
    return json.dumps(structured_parameters)
```
Note that this is a simplified implementation and may require additional logic and NLP techniques to handle more complex user requests.
