- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.

- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.

Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.

providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.

  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.
  example: User: "Triage my inbox" -> actions: INBOX
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling negotiation coordinator. Use this only for an existing proposal workflow: start a negotiation record, submit a concrete proposal for that negotiation, record accepted/declined responses, finalize the winning proposal, cancel, or list negotiations/proposals. Do not use this for first-turn calendar requests, recurring blocks, travel-time bundling, missed-call repair, or fresh candidate-slot searches; those belong to CALENDAR_ACTION, PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES, INBOX, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. Use for 'help me schedule a meeting with <person/team>', 'set up a sync with <person>', 'find a time with <team>' — subaction: start. Use for 'propose N times for a sync with <person>' — subaction: propose.
  aliases[5]: NEGOTIATE_MEETING, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING, MANAGE_SCHEDULING_NEGOTIATION, RESPOND_TO_MEETING_PROPOSAL, FINALIZE_SCHEDULING_NEGOTIATION
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- RUN_MORNING_CHECKIN: Run the morning check-in: assemble overdue todos, today's meetings, and yesterday's wins for the owner. Use this for a dedicated morning self-review/check-in, not for inbox daily briefs, unread cross-channel summaries, or reply workflows.
  aliases[3]: MORNING_CHECKIN, START_MORNING_CHECKIN, MORNING_ROUTINE_CHECKIN
- HEALTH: Query health and fitness data from HealthKit or Google Fit. Subactions: today, trend, by_metric, status.
  aliases[4]: FITNESS, HEALTHKIT, GOOGLE_FIT, WELLNESS
  params[5]: subaction?:string - Which health query to run: today, trend, by_metric, status.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; metric?:string - Metric for by_metric queries: steps, active_minutes, sleep_hours, heart_rate, calories, distance_meters.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for single-day queries.; days?:number - Window size for trend and by_metric queries.
  example: User: "How many steps did I take today?" -> actions: HEALTH
- SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS: Search across every connected channel — Gmail, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, WhatsApp, Signal, X DMs, Calendly — plus agent memory. Returns merged hits with citations to source platform, room, and timestamp. Connectors without native search emit typed unsupported markers (no fabricated results). Admin/owner only.
  aliases[5]: CROSS_CHANNEL_SEARCH, SEARCH_ALL_CHANNELS, SEARCH_EVERYWHERE, FIND_ACROSS_PLATFORMS, UNIFIED_SEARCH
  params[8]: query?:string - Exact search phrase. When omitted, the LLM extracts a query from `intent`.; intent?:string - Natural-language description of what to search for. Multilingual.; person?:string - Display name of the person to focus the search on. Resolves via the relationships graph when available.; primaryEntityId?:string - Pre-resolved canonical cluster primary entity id (UUID).; startIso?:string - Lower-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; endIso?:string - Upper-bound timestamp (ISO 8601). Optional.; channels?:array of string - Channel allowlist. Allowed values: gmail, memory, telegram, discord, imessage, whatsapp, signal, x-dm, calendly, calendar.; limit?:number - Per-channel hit cap (default 10).

  params[2]: entityId:string [examples="00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"] - The entity id to update.; updates:string [examples="[{\"name\":\"bio\",\"value\":\"Loves Rust\"}]"] - A JSON array of {"name": string, "value": string} field updates (stringified JSON).

This action can draft the call, ask which saved contact to use, and then require confirmation before dialing. If the user wants a real phone call to a third party, prefer this action over CALENDAR_ACTION, LIFE, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. The recipient must appear in the configured allow-list before the actual call is placed.

  params[3]: confirmed?:boolean - Must be true to actually place the call.; to?:string - E.164 phone number to call. Must appear in the external allow-list.; message?:string - Spoken message played when the recipient answers.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- BLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Start a local website block by editing the system hosts file. Use this for fixed-duration or generic focus blocks like 'block twitter and reddit for the next 2 hours', 'turn on a focus block for all social media sites', or 'block youtube'. Use recent conversation context to block public websites like x.com for a fixed duration or until manually unblocked. Do not use this when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, or todo; that is BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE. Always drafts first; the owner must pass confirmed: true (e.g. by replying 'confirm') to actually edit the hosts file. If the user confirms a block in a follow-up message without repeating the hostnames, reuse that context through the action planner. DO NOT use this action when the user references apps, games, or things 'on my phone' / 'on my device' — use BLOCK_APPS for those. Do not pair this action with a speculative REPLY; this action provides the final reply itself.

  params[3]: websites?:array of string - Website hostnames or URLs to block, for example ['x.com', 'twitter.com']. When omitted, the planner can recover them from recent conversation context.; durationMinutes?:number [default=60] - How long to block those websites, in minutes. Omit this to use the default duration.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, the action returns a draft confirmation request instead of editing the system hosts file.

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; thresholdDays:number - Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.
  example: User: "Remind me to follow up with Dana every 14 days" -> actions: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
- LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS: List currently active remote-control sessions.
  aliases[3]: REMOTE_SESSIONS, ACTIVE_REMOTE_SESSIONS, REMOTE_LIST
  example: User: "Are any remote sessions open?" -> actions: LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS
- LIST_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: List effective autofill whitelist entries: the bundled defaults plus user-added entries.
  aliases[2]: SHOW_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, GET_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Use this for real phone calls, not for calendar-only rescheduling or advice. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[6]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_SUPPORT
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold. Use this for overdue or pending follow-up list queries, not for scheduling a new reminder. Returns an empty list when the RelationshipsService is not available.
  aliases[5]: OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, WHO_TO_FOLLOW_UP, WHO_HAVEN_T_I_TALKED_TO, LIST_FOLLOWUPS, FOLLOWUP_LIST
  example: User: "Who should I follow up with?" -> actions: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
- CHECK_AVAILABILITY: Check whether the owner is free or busy across a specific ISO-8601 time window. Returns a free/busy summary and any overlapping events.
  aliases[3]: AM_I_FREE, AVAILABILITY_CHECK, FREE_BUSY
  params[2]: startAt:string - ISO-8601 start of the window to check.; endAt:string - ISO-8601 end of the window to check.
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.
  aliases[7]: SET_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SAVE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SET_PREFERRED_TIMES, SET_BLACKOUT_WINDOWS, SLEEP_WINDOW, NO_CALL_HOURS, PROTECT_SLEEP
  params[6]: timeZone?:string - IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.; preferredStartLocal?:string - Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; preferredEndLocal?:string - Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; defaultDurationMinutes?:number - Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).; travelBufferMinutes?:number - Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).; blackoutWindows?:array - Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
  example: User: "No calls between 11pm and 8am unless I explicitly say it's okay." -> actions: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
- PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES: Propose candidate meeting time slots to offer to another person. Reads the owner's calendar busy times and meeting preferences (preferred hours, blackout windows, travel buffer) and returns three available slots by default over the next seven days. Use this for bundled scheduling while traveling or when you need concrete reschedule options. DO NOT use for small talk, weather, general conversation, or vague mentions of travel. Only fire when the user explicitly asks to propose / suggest / offer time slots to another person (e.g. 'propose three times for a meeting with Marco').
  aliases[5]: SUGGEST_MEETING_TIMES, OFFER_MEETING_SLOTS, FIND_MEETING_SLOTS, PROPOSE_SLOTS, BUNDLE_MEETINGS_WHILE_TRAVELING
  params[5]: durationMinutes?:number - Meeting length in minutes. Defaults to the owner's configured default duration.; daysAhead?:number - Number of days ahead to search. Defaults to 7. Ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied.; slotCount?:number - Number of candidate slots to return. Defaults to 3.; windowStart?:string - Optional ISO-8601 earliest start for the search window.; windowEnd?:string - Optional ISO-8601 latest end for the search window.
  example: User: "While I'm traveling, try to bundle meetings with PendingReality and Ryan on the same day if possible." -> actions: PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES
- GET_TIME_ON_SITE: T8d — Time on a specific site based on browser activity reports pushed into the runtime store.

- BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'. Creates a block rule whose release is gated on todo completion. If todoName is provided with no matching active todo, the todo is created first. Do not use this for fixed-duration blocks like 'for 2 hours' or generic focus blocks like 'turn on social media blocking' — those are BLOCK_WEBSITES.
  aliases[5]: BLOCK_SITES_UNTIL_TODO_DONE, BLOCK_WEBSITE_UNTIL_TASK, CONDITIONAL_WEBSITE_BLOCK, BLOCK_UNTIL_DONE, FOCUS_UNTIL_TASK_DONE
  params[5]: websites:array of string - List of website hostnames to block.; todoId?:string - ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.; todoName?:string - Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.; unlockDurationMinutes?:number - Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.; profile?:string - Optional profile label for the block rule.
  example: User: "Block x.com until I finish my workout." -> actions: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
- DOSSIER: Generate a pre-meeting or person-background briefing dossier with context about attendees, recent interactions, and upcoming event details. Use this for requests like 'pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella', 'give me the background on the person I'm meeting next: Julia Chen', or 'brief me for my next meeting'. If the user explicitly wants a brief, backgrounder, prep sheet, or dossier, use this action instead of replying from ENTITIES, FACTS, or memory alone.

  params[5]: intent?:string - Natural language request. Examples: "brief me for my 2pm with Alice", "prep me for tomorrow's board meeting".; calendarEventId?:string - Optional calendar event id to pull event details (title, time, location, attendees).; subject?:string - Subject line for the dossier (e.g. meeting title or topic).; attendeeHandles?:array of string - List of attendee handles/emails to look up in the relationships table.; generatedForAt?:string - ISO timestamp the dossier is generated for (defaults to now).
  example: User: "Pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella" -> actions: DOSSIER
- GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT: T8d — Per-app time breakdown for the last N hours (default 24h). Returns noDataReason='macos-only' on non-Darwin platforms.
  aliases[3]: ACTIVITY_REPORT, WHAT_DID_I_WORK_ON, TIME_TRACKING_REPORT

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Use search_events for flights, itinerary, travel, appointments, or keyword lookup; feed for agenda/schedule reads; next_event for the next upcoming event; create_event only when creating a new event.; intent?:string - Natural language calendar request, especially schedule or itinerary questions. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION
- REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION: Admin-only. Prepare local website blocking by requesting administrator/root approval when the machine supports it, or explain the manual change needed when it does not.
  aliases[5]: ENABLE_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, ALLOW_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, GRANT_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_SELFCONTROL_PERMISSION

- RELATIONSHIP: Manage contacts, relationships, and follow-ups. Subactions: list_contacts, add_contact, log_interaction, add_follow_up, complete_follow_up, follow_up_list, days_since. Use for 'remind me to follow up with <person> [when]', 'add a follow-up with <person>', 'schedule a check-in with <person>' — subaction: add_follow_up. Use for 'how long since I talked to <person>' — subaction: days_since. Use for 'who do I need to follow up with' — subaction: follow_up_list.

When relationshipId is omitted, the handler resolves an existing contact by this name.; channel?:string - Primary channel for the contact (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, x_dm).; handle?:string - Primary handle/address on the chosen channel.; email?:string - Optional email address for the contact.; phone?:string - Optional phone number for the contact.; notes?:string - Free-form notes or interaction summary.; relationshipId?:string - Target relationship id.; followUpId?:string - Target follow-up id.; reason?:string - Reason or purpose for a follow-up.; dueAt?:string - Follow-up due time. Accepts natural language like 'tomorrow', 'next week', or 'next Tuesday at 3pm', or an ISO-8601 timestamp.; confirmed?:boolean - Optional explicit confirmation flag.

- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.
  aliases[3]: STOP_TALKING, STOP_CHATTING, STOP_CONVERSATION
  example: User: "Go screw yourself" -> actions: IGNORE
- SCREEN_TIME: Query screen time summaries (per app, per website, daily). Use this for quantitative usage questions like 'how much screen time have I used today?', 'break down my screen time by app this week', or 'which websites did I spend the most time on?'. Do not use this when the user is only reflecting or venting like 'I spend too much time on my phone' unless they actually ask for the numbers. Subactions: summary, today, weekly, by_app, by_website.
  aliases[4]: SCREENTIME, APP_USAGE, WEBSITE_USAGE, DWELL_TIME
  params[6]: subaction?:string - Which screen time query to run: summary, today, weekly, by_app, by_website.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not explicitly set.; source?:string - Restrict to 'app' or 'website'.; identifier?:string - Specific app bundle id or website domain when filtering to a single source.; date?:string - YYYY-MM-DD for 'today' / daily queries.; sinceDays?:number - Number of days back from now for summary windows.

- CALL_USER: Place an outbound phone call to the agent owner via Twilio. Use this when the assistant is blocked and needs real-time help from the owner, or when the owner explicitly asks to be called. This action can be selected on a standing escalation policy or first-turn draft; without confirmation it returns a confirmation request instead of dialing.

- UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE: Silently persist stable, owner-only LifeOps profile details when the canonical owner clearly states or confirms them. Use only for the owner, never for other contacts, and do not ask follow-up questions just to fill these fields. Do not use this for todos, goals, reminders, temporary plans, or live task state.

- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.
  aliases[14]: CHECK_INBOX, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_DIGEST, INBOX_RESPOND, TRIAGE_INBOX, SCAN_MESSAGES, CHECK_MESSAGES, DAILY_DIGEST, DAILY_BRIEF, INBOX_SUMMARY, REPLY_INBOX, RESPOND_TO_MESSAGE, MISSED_CALL_FOLLOWUP, GROUP_CHAT_HANDOFF
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.
  example: User: "Triage my inbox" -> actions: INBOX
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling negotiation coordinator. Use this only for an existing proposal workflow: start a negotiation record, submit a concrete proposal for that negotiation, record accepted/declined responses, finalize the winning proposal, cancel, or list negotiations/proposals. Do not use this for first-turn calendar requests, recurring blocks, travel-time bundling, missed-call repair, or fresh candidate-slot searches; those belong to CALENDAR_ACTION, PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES, INBOX, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. Use for 'help me schedule a meeting with <person/team>', 'set up a sync with <person>', 'find a time with <team>' — subaction: start. Use for 'propose N times for a sync with <person>' — subaction: propose.
  aliases[5]: NEGOTIATE_MEETING, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING, MANAGE_SCHEDULING_NEGOTIATION, RESPOND_TO_MEETING_PROPOSAL, FINALIZE_SCHEDULING_NEGOTIATION
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- RUN_MORNING_CHECKIN: Run the morning check-in: assemble overdue todos, today's meetings, and yesterday's wins for the owner. Use this for a dedicated morning self-review/check-in, not for inbox daily briefs, unread cross-channel summaries, or reply workflows.
  aliases[3]: MORNING_CHECKIN, START_MORNING_CHECKIN, MORNING_ROUTINE_CHECKIN

- CALL_EXTERNAL: Place an outbound phone call to a third party via Twilio. Use this for approved booking, reschedule, outage, support, or escalation calls to vendors or counterparties. Examples: 'call the dentist and reschedule my appointment', 'phone my cable company and ask about the outage', 'call the airline', or 'call the hotel to rebook'. This action can draft the call, ask which saved contact to use, and then require confirmation before dialing. If the user wants a real phone call to a third party, prefer this action over CALENDAR_ACTION, LIFE, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. The recipient must appear in the configured allow-list before the actual call is placed.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

actions[58]:

  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- INTENT_SYNC: Broadcast intents across devices or acknowledge pending intents. Subactions: broadcast, list_pending, acknowledge, prune_expired.
  aliases[3]: BROADCAST_INTENT, SYNC_INTENT, CROSS_DEVICE_INTENT
  params[12]: subaction:string [values=broadcast|list_pending|acknowledge|prune_expired] - Which operation to perform. One of: broadcast, list_pending, acknowledge, prune_expired.; intent?:string - User-phrased intent summary (optional, for logging).; kind?:string [values=user_action_requested|routine_reminder|attention_request|state_sync] - Intent kind. One of: user_action_requested, routine_reminder, attention_request, state_sync.; title?:string - Short intent title (shown in notification).; body?:string - Intent body text.; target?:string [values=all|desktop|mobile|specific] - Target device class. One of: all, desktop, mobile, specific. Defaults to "all".; targetDeviceId?:string - Specific device id when target = 'specific'.; actionUrl?:string - Deep link URL for mobile follow-up.; priority?:string [values=low|medium|high|urgent] - Priority. One of: low, medium, high, urgent. Defaults to "medium".; intentId?:string - Intent id to acknowledge.; deviceId?:string - Device id performing the acknowledgement, or requesting pending intents.; expiresInMinutes?:number - Expire the intent after this many minutes.
  example: User: "Ping my phone to take out the trash" -> actions: INTENT_SYNC
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser. Use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead for Finder/Desktop automation, screenshots, folder creation, or local file workflows on this machine.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE
  params[3]: ruleId:string - ID of the block rule to release.; confirmed:boolean - Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.; reason?:string - Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.
  example: User: "Release the block rule I just created." -> actions: RELEASE_BLOCK
- MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE: Mark a contact as already followed-up-with (updates lastContactedAt to now). Use this only when the interaction already happened, not for future reminders. Requires either an explicit contactId (UUID) or an unambiguous contactName. Ambiguous names return a clarifying response without modifying any contact.
  aliases[5]: FOLLOWED_UP, FOLLOWUP_DONE, CONTACTED, MARK_CONTACTED, RECORD_INTERACTION
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known — eliminates name ambiguity.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; note?:string - Optional note about the interaction.
  example: User: "I just talked to Alice, mark it done" -> actions: MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE
- BLOCK_APPS: Admin-only. Block selected apps on the user's phone using native OS controls. On iPhone, uses Family Controls to shield apps. On Android, uses Usage Access to detect and overlay blocked apps. Use this for requests like 'block all games on my phone until 6pm' or 'block the Slack app while I focus on deep work'. Pass app package names (Android) or previously selected app tokens (iPhone) to block.
  aliases[6]: BLOCK_APP, BLOCK_APPLICATION, APP_BLOCKER, START_APP_BLOCK, BLOCK_DISTRACTING_APPS, SHIELD_APPS
  params[3]: packageNames?:array of string - Android package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android', 'com.instagram.android'].   example: User: "Block Twitter and Instagram for 2 hours." -> actions: BLOCK_APPS
- PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT: Publish a cross-device intent (alarm, reminder, block, or custom) to the device bus so all paired devices can realize it. - BLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Start a local website block by editing the system hosts file. - LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for person-specific follow-ups like 'remind me to follow up with David next week about the project' — use RELATIONSHIP instead. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action. DO NOT use this action when the user asks to 'help schedule' / 'set up' / 'find a time for' a meeting with a person or team without a specific time — that is SCHEDULING (subaction start). DO NOT use this action to 'propose', 'suggest', or 'offer' meeting time slots — that is PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use CALENDAR_ACTION only when the user specifies (or intends to specify) a concrete date/time for the event.
